prompt: This section is the Document Title. It contains exactly: the title text at the very top of the document preceding the date, indicating the nature of the document and the opposing party (e.g., 'Demand Letter against [opposing_party_name]'). Do NOT include the date, delivery method, recipient name, address, or any subsequent header information. DATA FROM FIELD DATA ONLY: All variable facts (such as the opposing party's name) must come from the provided field data. If a piece of data is missing, output the exact string '[field_name]'. Do not invent or hallucinate any facts, names, or details. OUTPUT: Provide only the actual document text for this section. Do not include any summaries, introductory phrases, or meta-commentary. LEGAL PROSE: Use standard formal legal phrasing for a document title; do not use bullets or markdown. ONE SECTION ONLY: You must output ONLY the content for this specific section and stop immediately before the Date or Header section begins.

You are an expert legal drafter. Generate ONLY the 'Date and Delivery Method' section of a Pre-Litigation Demand Letter.

SCOPE: This section is the Date and Delivery Method. It contains exactly: the date of the letter and the specified method of delivery (e.g., 'BY E-MAIL'). Do NOT include the recipient's name, title, company, address, subject line, or any subsequent header information.

DATA: All variable facts (dates, delivery methods) must come from the provided Field data. If a required fact is missing, output the exact placeholder [field_name]. Do not invent any facts, dates, or delivery methods.

OUTPUT: Output only the actual document text for this section. No summaries, no meta-commentary, and no markdown formatting. 

FORMAT: Output the date on the first line (either as 'DATE: [letter_date]' or simply '[letter_date]') and the delivery method on the second line (e.g., 'BY [delivery_method]').

STOP: Stop generating immediately after the delivery method line. Do not generate the recipient block or any other following sections.

You are generating the 'Recipient Information' section of a Pre-Litigation Demand Letter. This section contains exactly: the recipient's name, title, organization, and mailing address formatted as a standard mailing block. Do NOT include the date, delivery method, subject/reference line, or salutation, as those belong to other sections.

Use ONLY the provided field data for all variable facts. If a field is missing, output the exact placeholder [field_name]. Do not invent any names, titles, or addresses.

Output ONLY the document text for this section. Provide no summaries, introductions, or meta-commentary. Format the output as a standard multi-line address block (e.g., Name on the first line, Title on the second, Organization on the third, Address on the subsequent lines). Do not use bullets or markdown formatting. Stop generating immediately after the final line of the address.

This section is the Reference Line. It contains exactly: the subject line starting with 'Re:' followed by a brief description of the legal matter or claim and the name of the opposing organization or location where the incident occurred. Do NOT include the header, recipient address, salutation, or any introductory paragraphs that belong to the previous or next sections.

Use ONLY the provided field data. All variable facts must come from the field data. If a field is missing, output the exact placeholder [field_name]. Do not invent facts.

Output ONLY the actual document text for this section. No summaries, no meta-commentary. Document text only.

Format the output as a single line of formal legal text exactly like this: 'Re: [claim_type] at [opposing_organization_name]'.

Stop generating immediately after this single line. Do not spill over into the salutation or introduction.

This section is the Epigraph / Corporate Policy Quote. It contains exactly: a direct quotation from the opposing party's corporate policy or relevant document, followed by the attribution or title of that policy. Do NOT include the Header, Subject Line, Salutation, or any other part of the Pre-Litigation Demand Letter.

DATA FROM FIELD DATA ONLY: All variable facts come from Field data. Missing → [field_name]. No invented facts.

OUTPUT: Only the actual document text for this section. No summaries, no meta-commentary. Document text only.

FORMATTING: Output the exact quote provided in the [corporate_policy_quote] field enclosed in double quotation marks. On the next line, output a hyphen followed by the [corporate_policy_source]. Do not use markdown, bolding, or bullet points.

ONE SECTION ONLY: Output ONLY the content for this section and stop immediately after the policy source.

This section is the Salutation. It contains exactly: the formal greeting to the recipient. Do NOT include the Header, Subject/Reference Line, Introduction, or any other preceding or succeeding sections.

DATA FROM FIELD DATA ONLY: All variable facts must come from the provided field data. If a field is missing, output exactly the bracketed field name (e.g., [recipient_last_name]). Do not invent facts.

OUTPUT: Only the actual document text for this section. No summaries, no meta-commentary. Document text only.

FORMAT: The output must be a single line formatted exactly as 'Dear [recipient_title] [recipient_last_name],'. Ensure the comma is included at the end.

This section is the 'Introduction and Representation'. It contains exactly: the opening statement of the law firm's representation of the client, a brief introduction of the client's background and protected class (if applicable), historical or legal context of the relevant laws (if applicable to the claims), a general introduction to the core claims against the defendant, a summary list of the specific state and federal laws violated, and a transitional sentence introducing the upcoming factual narrative. Do NOT include the header, salutation, detailed factual background/timeline, specific settlement demands, or spoliation notice. Use ONLY the provided field data for variable facts. If a field is missing, use the exact placeholder [field_name]. Do not invent facts, dates, or names. Output ONLY the continuous formal legal prose for this section. Do not use markdown formatting, bullet points, summaries, or meta-commentary. Adapt the legal language and historical context to fit the specific case type and claims provided in the field data. Stop generating immediately after the transitional sentence that leads into the factual background.

You are an expert legal drafter. Generate the 'Timeline of Discriminatory Conduct' section for a Pre-Litigation Demand Letter.

1. DEFINE SECTION SCOPE EXPLICITLY: This section is the Timeline of Discriminatory Conduct. It contains exactly: a chronological, day-by-day narrative of the opposing party's discriminatory, retaliatory, and unlawful actions against the client, including specific interactions, dates, and actors involved. Do NOT include the Introduction, Legal Claims, Conclusion and Demand, or Spoliation Notice.

2. DATA FROM FIELD DATA ONLY: All variable facts (names, dates, vehicle details, amounts, actors) must come from the provided Field data. If a piece of data is missing, output the exact placeholder [field_name]. Do not invent any facts, dates, or names.

3. OUTPUT: Provide only the actual document text for this section. Start exactly with the heading 'Timeline of Discriminatory Conduct' followed by the chronological paragraphs. Do not include any summaries, meta-commentary, or extra text outside the section.

4. LEGAL PROSE: Write in continuous formal legal prose. Format each entry as a new paragraph starting with the date (e.g., '[event_date_1]: [Description]'). Do not use bullet points or markdown lists. Adapt the legal language to highlight the specific causes of action (e.g., racial animus, retaliation, breach of agreement) as dictated by the field data.

5. ONE SECTION ONLY: Output ONLY the content for this section and stop immediately before the 'Legal Claims' section.

This section is the Factual Background. It contains exactly: the unheaded narrative paragraphs outlining the chronological series of concerning incidents, harassment, or other actionable conduct, and the timeline of events leading up to the legal claims. Do NOT include the Introduction (statement of representation) or the Legal Claims (identification of specific laws violated), which belong to the previous and next sections respectively.

DATA FROM FIELD DATA ONLY: All variable facts (names, dates, incidents, employer, opposing party) must come from the provided Field data. If a piece of data is missing, output the exact placeholder: [field_name]. Do not invent any facts, dates, or names.

OUTPUT: Generate only the actual document text for this section. Do not include any summaries, introductory filler, or meta-commentary. Document text only.

LEGAL PROSE: Write in continuous, formal legal prose. Do not use bullet points or markdown formatting. Adapt the legal language and narrative flow to the specific case type and facts provided in the field data (e.g., if it is a personal injury or discrimination case rather than harassment, adjust the narrative focus accordingly).

ONE SECTION ONLY: Output ONLY the content for the Factual Background section. Stop immediately after concluding the factual narrative and before any numbered sections or headings for Legal Claims.

You are an expert legal drafter. Generate ONLY the section titled 'II. Inconsistencies with Findings' for a Pre-Litigation Demand Letter.

SCOPE: This section is 'II. Inconsistencies with Findings'. It contains exactly: a narrative detailing the inconsistencies in the disciplinary findings regarding the accused; concerns regarding transparency and the accused's potential return to work at the organization; a description of the compelling evidence (e.g., screenshots, explicit messages) presented by the client during the proceedings; the specific guilty verdict, including the adjudicating body, number of counts, fine amount, and mandated training hours for the specific claim type; the discrepancy between the initial disciplinary action (e.g., termination) and the final measures; the lack of clarity from the union/organization; and the resulting uncertainty and continued uneasiness felt by the client. Do NOT include the general factual background/timeline (which belongs in the previous section) or the formal legal claims and financial demands (which belong in subsequent sections).

DATA: Use ONLY the provided field data for variable facts. If a field is missing, output exactly the bracketed field name (e.g., [client_last_name]). Do not invent any facts, names, amounts, or entities.

FORMATTING & STYLE: Output ONLY the actual document text for this section. Begin exactly with the heading 'II. Inconsistencies with Findings'. Use continuous, formal legal prose. Do not use bullet points or markdown formatting. Do not include any summaries, introductory remarks, or meta-commentary. Stop generating immediately after concluding the narrative about the client's continued uneasiness and the organization's lack of a definitive stance.

You are drafting a specific section of a Pre-Litigation Demand Letter. This section is titled 'III. Lack of Termination shows room for Negligence Retention'. It contains exactly: 1) A narrative explaining when the harassment began and how the harasser obtained the plaintiff's contact information (including how they obtained a changed phone number); 2) A description of the duration of the harassment and the employer/union's failure to permanently terminate the harasser, highlighting the leniency that leaves the door open for the harasser's return and the plaintiff's resulting fear; 3) The date and details of the plaintiff escalating the matter to the local police department due to the severe impact on their well-being. Do NOT include the general factual background, formal statutory legal claims, spoliation notices, or settlement demands, as those belong in previous or subsequent sections. Use ONLY the provided field data for variable facts. If a field is missing, use its exact placeholder [field_name]. Do not invent facts, dates, or names. Output ONLY the actual document text for this section in continuous, formal legal prose. Do not use bullet points, summaries, or meta-commentary. Stop generating immediately after concluding the paragraph about the police involvement.

This section is the 'Legal Violations Assertion' section. It contains exactly: the heading '[defendant_name]’s Conduct Violates the Law', followed by a brief introductory paragraph asserting that the opposing party's conduct illustrates a disregard for the law, and a specific statement that the entity's conduct toward the plaintiff violated the laws of the United States and the specific state jurisdiction. Do NOT include the factual background, detailed statutory analysis, or the settlement demand, which belong to previous or subsequent sections.

Data from Field Data Only. Use the following fields to populate the variables:
- Defendant Name: [defendant_name]
- Defendant Entity Type (e.g., Dealership, Company, Corporation): [defendant_entity_type]
- Plaintiff Name: [plaintiff_name]
- State of Jurisdiction: [jurisdiction_state]

If any field is missing, output the exact placeholder (e.g., '[defendant_name]'). Do not invent facts.

Output ONLY the actual document text for this section. Write in continuous formal legal prose. Do not use bullet points or markdown. Ensure grammatical correctness (e.g., 'conduct toward [plaintiff_name] violated the laws' rather than 'was violated'). Stop generating immediately after this paragraph; do not generate the next section's heading or content. No summaries or meta-commentary.

You are an expert legal drafter. Generate the 'Legal Claims' section of a Pre-Litigation Demand Letter.

This section is titled 'IV. [defendant_name]’s Conduct Violates the Law'. It contains exactly: a brief, forceful assertion that the described incidents illustrate the defendant's continuing disregard for protecting its employees from the specific misconduct (e.g., sexual harassment, discrimination) perpetrated by the bad actor, and a concluding sentence stating that the failure to protect the victim from the bad actor's actions violates specific statutes or laws.

Do NOT include the factual background or timeline (which belongs to the previous section) and do NOT include the conclusion, settlement demand, or spoliation notice (which belong to the subsequent sections).

DATA FROM FIELD DATA ONLY: All variable facts must come from the provided field data. If a piece of data is missing, output the exact placeholder [field_name]. Do not invent any facts, names, or laws.

OUTPUT: Output ONLY the actual document text for this section, starting with the Roman numeral heading 'IV. [defendant_name]’s Conduct Violates the Law'. Provide no summaries, no meta-commentary, and no introductory remarks.

LEGAL PROSE: Use continuous, formal legal prose. Do not use bullet points or markdown formatting for the text body. Adapt the legal language and cause-of-action wording to fit the specific case type and applicable laws provided in the field data.

ONE SECTION ONLY: Output ONLY the content for this section and stop immediately. Do not generate any content for the next section.

This section is the 'Conclusion'. It contains exactly: a statement expressing the client's devastation caused by the opposing party's conduct, a declaration of the client's commitment to achieving complete vindication, a threat to commence litigation immediately with confidence in a large jury award (mentioning punitive damages and specific statutes if applicable), and a concluding sentence offering the letter as a good faith opportunity to resolve the matter without costly litigation. Do NOT include the detailed legal claims (which belong to the previous section) or the Spoliation/Preservation of Evidence Notice, deadlines, or signatures (which belong to subsequent sections).

DATA FROM FIELD DATA ONLY: All variable facts (names, opposing party, summary of mistreatment, statutes) come from Field data. Missing -> [field_name]. No invented facts.

OUTPUT: Only the actual document text for this section. No summaries, no meta-commentary. Document text only.

LEGAL PROSE: Continuous formal legal prose; no bullets or markdown. Adapt the description of the mistreatment and damages to the specific case type provided in the field data.

ONE SECTION ONLY: Output ONLY the content for this section and stop immediately. Do not generate the heading 'Spoliation Notice' or any subsequent content.

You are an expert legal drafter. Generate ONLY the 'Preservation of Evidence Notice' section of a Pre-Litigation Demand Letter.

SCOPE: This section is the Preservation of Evidence Notice. It contains exactly two paragraphs. The first paragraph reminds the opposing party of their legal obligation to take immediate steps to preserve any material related to the matter (including emails, instant messages, voicemails, text messages, documents, and electronically stored information in electronic or paper form). The second paragraph instructs them to preserve personal and business hard drives, back-up storage tapes, cellular telephones, computers, laptops, and other devices of all individuals potentially having knowledge related to the action. Do NOT include the preceding Conclusion and Demand section, and do NOT include the subsequent Deadline or Sign-off sections.

DATA: Use the provided field data for the opposing party's name. If missing, use the exact placeholder [opposing_party_name]. Do not invent any facts, names, or details.

OUTPUT FORMAT: Output only the continuous formal legal prose for these two paragraphs. Do not use bullet points, markdown formatting, or introductory/concluding remarks. Provide the document text only.

This section is the 'Deadline and Next Steps'. It contains exactly: a single paragraph specifying the exact date or deadline for the recipient to contact the firm for an amicable resolution before formal litigation is initiated, and a warning that failure to do so will result in the firm moving forward without further notice. Do NOT include the spoliation/preservation of evidence notice (which belongs to the previous section) or the sign-off and signature block (which belongs to the next section). DATA FROM FIELD DATA ONLY: All variable facts (specifically the deadline date) must come from the provided Field data. If a piece of data is missing, insert the placeholder [field_name]. Do not invent any facts or dates. OUTPUT: Generate only the actual document text for this section. No summaries, no meta-commentary, no markdown formatting, and no bullet points. LEGAL PROSE: Use continuous formal legal prose matching the tone of a pre-litigation demand letter. ONE SECTION ONLY: Output ONLY the content for this section and stop immediately before the sign-off.

This section is the Sign-off and Signature Block. It contains exactly: the closing phrase (e.g., 'Yours truly,') and the attorney's name. Do NOT include the deadline, spoliation notice, or any preceding sections. All variable facts (such as the attorney's name) come from Field data. If a field is missing, output the exact placeholder [field_name]. Do not invent any facts. Output ONLY the actual document text for this section. No summaries, no meta-commentary, and no markdown formatting. Provide continuous formal legal prose appropriate for a sign-off. Output ONLY the content for this section and stop immediately after the attorney's name.
